texto: If you put these five things together - you can't use money to attract talent, you can't advertise, you can't take risks, you can't invest in long-term results, and you don't have a stock market - then we have just put the humanitarian sector at the most extreme disadvantage to the for-profit sector on every level, and then we call the whole system charity, as if there is something incredibly sweet about it.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Dan Pallotta: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “If you put these five things together - you can't use money to attract talent, you can't advertis…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que negocios, charity, fundraising pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de fundraising e non-profit.

texto: It has been said that love is a function of communication. I believe that to be true. I believe, by extension, that human understanding is a function of communication. And the better human beings understand one another, the higher the level of functioning.
explicacao: Citação de Dan Pallotta sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “It has been said that love is a function of communication. I believe that to be true. I believe, …” articula amor, love, love-is em registro de amor, com relevância para contextos como love-is e believe.

frase_original: It has been said that love is a function of communication. I believe that to be true. I believe, by extension, that human understanding is a function of communication. And the better human beings understand one another, the higher the level of functioning.

texto: You used to be able to just call people. You didn't have to be on someone's calendar to have a phone conversation. The telephone was an important and valuable domain of communication, both for casual, friendly chats and for professional exchanges of ideas and information. But no more.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Dan Pallotta sobre temas ligados a amizade. A afirmação “You used to be able to just call people. You didn't have to be on someone's calendar to have a ph…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a amizade, ideas, people, útil em momentos de mudanca e termino.

frase_original: You used to be able to just call people. You didn't have to be on someone's calendar to have a phone conversation. The telephone was an important and valuable domain of communication, both for casual, friendly chats and for professional exchanges of ideas and information. But no more.
